summary: Removed remaining or many `MUST_MATCH` conditionals from `src/melee` by either keeping the required C tricks unconditionally, converting matched functions to normal C, renaming numeric library/fighter symbols to semantic names, and moving `lb_00B0` back to an asm object where its inline-asm/placeholder state was not cleanly compilable as source. The PR was merged without discussion or review comments, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Removed `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` branches and empty guards across multiple `src/melee` files, including fighter common code, character init/special files, ground, collision, item, and map collision code.
Changed `obj_files.mk` to build `asm/melee/lb/lb_00B0.s.o` instead of `src/melee/lb/lb_00B0.c.o`, while adding a full `asm/melee/lb/lb_00B0.s` translation unit.

Kept some matching-only source patterns unconditionally instead of hiding them behind `MUST_MATCH`, such as repeated assignment in Game & Watch neutral special, unused stack locals in Sheik/ground/lbcollision/walk code, and `#pragma force_active` blocks for Zako Boy/Girl demo strings.

Renamed several address-style library functions to descriptive names in source, headers, symbols, and asm references: `lb_8000BA44` to `lbDObjSetRateAll`, `lb_8000BB24` to `lbDObjReqAnimAll`, `lb_8000BD28` to `lbGetJObjFramerate`, `lb_8000BDB4` to `lbGetJObjCurrFrame`, `lb_8000BE40` to `lbGetJObjEndFrame`, `lb_8000CC5C` to `lbGetFreeColorRegister`, and `lb_8000CD60` to `lbGetFreeAlphaRegister`.
Renamed `ftCo_GetParasolStatus` to `ftGetParasolStatus` across C, headers, dox, symbol map, and asm call sites, preserving the `@was{ftGetParasolStatus}` breadcrumb in `ftcommon.c`.
Introduced `src/melee/mp/forward.h` with `mpLib_Callback` and adjusted `mplib.h`/`mpcoll.c` callback typing.
Replaced a Kirby unknown typedef from `unk_t ftKirby_UnkArrayThing[12]` with an explicit 12-field struct and used `ftKirby_UnkArrayThing*` in `ftKb_Init_800EE528`.
Added missing baselib includes such as `dobj.h`, `tobj.h`, and `gobj.h` where semantic calls or declarations required them.
smart_moves: Used an asm fallback for `lb_00B0` at the object-file level rather than preserving large inline-asm `MUST_MATCH` blocks inside C; this makes source files cleaner while keeping the build matched.
Made necessary dummy stack variables and odd matching constructs unconditional, which removes preprocessor noise without pretending those constructs are ordinary logic.

Renamed numeric symbols only where behavior was already evident from wrappers/call sites, making downstream C more readable while synchronizing asm references and `symbols.txt` so links remain stable.

Kept alternative matching attempts behind local `#define SOLUTION` switches in a few places (`ftCommon_8007CEF4`, `mpColl_80043558`) instead of project-wide `MUST_MATCH`, making the remaining matching issue localized.
Preserved matching-sensitive asserts by using fixed line numbers where needed rather than relying on conditional `MUST_MATCH` assert aliases.
decomp_lessons: If a file still depends heavily on raw asm or nonmatching placeholders, moving that translation unit to `asm/` can be cleaner than carrying giant inline asm guarded by `MUST_MATCH` in C.

Remove global matching macros by converting each case to an explicit local tactic: dummy stack locals, repeated assignments, fixed assert line numbers, force-active pragmas, or a localized solution selector.
When renaming an address-style function, update every dependent layer together: source calls, headers/dox, asm branch labels/calls, and `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`.

Semantic names can often be inferred safely from wrapper behavior: JObj frame/rate helpers, DObj animation helpers, and TEV color/alpha register allocators were renamed consistently with their actual use.
Structuring unknown arrays as named structs can improve typing without requiring full semantic field names; the Kirby 12-word array became an explicit `ftKirby_UnkArrayThing` with offset fields.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_GetParasolStatus` was shortened/generalized to `ftGetParasolStatus`, implying parasol status is not treated as strictly `ftCo_`-scoped.
Library helper names use descriptive `lb` prefixes plus object domain/action: `lbGetJObjCurrFrame`, `lbGetJObjEndFrame`, `lbGetJObjFramerate`, `lbDObjSetRateAll`, `lbDObjReqAnimAll`.
Register allocation helpers use explicit TEV purpose names: `lbGetFreeColorRegister` and `lbGetFreeAlphaRegister`.
Unknown structs may use a local descriptive placeholder plus offset-based fields, as in `ftKirby_UnkArrayThing` with `x0`, `x4`, ..., `x2C`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced `src/melee/lb/lb_00B0.c.o` with `asm/melee/lb/lb_00B0.s.o` in `obj_files.mk` while keeping a cleaned-up C file for reference/non-built source.
Updated asm call targets and relocation references to renamed symbols, e.g. `bl ftGetParasolStatus`, `bl lbGetJObjEndFrame`, and `.rel ftGetParasolStatus`.
Left unused stack locals such as `u32 _[6]`, `u32 _[8]`, `u32 _[5]`, and unused pointer temporaries directly in C to preserve stack layout without macro guards.
Used fixed-line `HSD_ASSERT(1276, ftGetParasolStatus(gobj) != FtParasol_None)` and `HSD_ASSERT(299/308, ...)` patterns instead of conditional assert wrappers.
Kept small artificial constructs like `fp = fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj);` where needed for matching rather than hiding them behind `#ifdef MUST_MATCH`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the PR slice.
The PR carried the `code-quality` label in GitHub metadata.
Because there was no human discussion, reviewer intent and rationale beyond the title/diff are not evidenced.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that restore `src/melee/lb/lb_00B0.c.o` after matching the C source.
Search for remaining `#define SOLUTION` blocks in `src/melee` after PR 1178.
Search for residual `MUST_MATCH` outside `src/melee` or newly introduced guards after this cleanup.
Audit all call sites of `ftGetParasolStatus` after the rename for stale `ftCo_GetParasolStatus` references.
